evidence: handleFullSketchForType (surface branch) builds relation entries with keys {name, cql, collocate_pos_group, collocations} — no 'id' or 'total_matches'
handleRelationQueryForPattern (single-relation, both SURFACE and DEP) builds entries with {id, name, collocations, total_matches} — no 'cql' or 'collocate_pos_group'
buildRelationResponse (DEP full-sketch) returns {id, name, description, deprel, total_matches, collocations} — a third distinct shape
All three paths place their result under the same outer key 'relations' in the response map
handleBcqlQueryPost sets response keys query/total_results/limit/results with no 'status' field; every other success handler sets 'status': 'ok'
handleConcordanceExamples sets 'limit_requested' (line 305); handleBcqlQueryPost sets 'limit' (line 382) for the same concept

QueryExecutor declares findCollocations(String lemma, String cqlPattern, double minLogDice, int maxResults)->List<WordSketchResult> and executeSurfacePattern(String lemma, String bcqlPattern, double minLogDice, int maxResults)->List<WordSketchResult> — identical Java signatures, incompatible pattern conventions
wrapHandler in WordSketchApiServer catches both IOException and Exception and sends uniform 500 JSON for every registered handler
HttpApiUtils provides sendJsonResponse, sendError, requireParam, parseQueryParams applied identically across all handlers
ExploreOptions record bundles 5 tuning parameters; private ExploreParams inner record in ExplorationHandlers mirrors this at the HTTP layer
QueryExecutor interface parameter ordering is consistently (lemma, pattern, minLogDice, maxResults) across all query methods

strengths: wrapHandler provides uniform IOException + Exception catching and consistent 500 JSON responses across every registered endpoint — no ad-hoc error divergence
HttpApiUtils is a narrow, uniformly-applied toolkit: CORS headers, content-type, and response-code wiring live in one place and are called identically from all handlers
ExploreOptions record and the private ExploreParams inner record prevent bloated method signatures at both the service and HTTP layers
QueryExecutor interface methods follow a consistent (lemma, pattern, minLogDice, maxResults) parameter order with every method declaring throws IOException
Handler split into SketchHandlers / ExplorationHandlers provides a clear, coherent boundary between sketch/concordance concerns and semantic-field exploration
api_surface_coherence.issue_character: Issues are spread across the public response layer and the primary query interface, reflecting incremental extension of the API surface rather than a single structural flaw: shape divergence accumulated as new endpoints reused the same key names with different field sets.
api_surface_coherence.score_rationale: The HTTP infrastructure layer (utilities, error handling, parameter objects) is solid and uniformly applied, which lifts this above the 60 anchor. However, the three differently-shaped objects all returned under the same 'relations' key across full-sketch, single-relation, and dependency endpoints is a genuine API contract problem — a client cannot use a single model type. The missing 'status' field on the BCQL endpoint and the 'limit'/'limit_requested' divergence add to the friction. The QueryExecutor overload problem (identical Java signatures with incompatible pattern conventions) adds implementation-level risk for future contributors. Together these issues place the codebase in the 60–65 band: solid plumbing, uneven data contract.

issues.summary: Surface full-sketch, single-relation, and DEP full-sketch all return differently-shaped objects under the same 'relations' key
evidence: Surface full-sketch (handleFullSketchForType, isDep=false) emits: {name, cql, collocate_pos_group, collocations} — no 'id' or 'total_matches'
Single-relation surface query (handleRelationQueryForPattern) emits: {id, name, collocations, total_matches} — no 'cql' or 'collocate_pos_group'
DEP full-sketch (buildRelationResponse) emits: {id, name, description, deprel, total_matches, collocations} — a third distinct shape
All three are placed under the outer key 'relations' in the response
issues.suggestion: Define a canonical relation-entry shape for surface responses — at minimum, include 'id' and 'total_matches' in the surface full-sketch branch (handleFullSketchForType isDep=false) so it matches the single-relation branch. Consider a shared formatRelationEntry helper instead of three separate ad-hoc map constructions.

issues.summary: POST /api/bcql response omits the 'status' field present in every other endpoint's success response
evidence: handleBcqlQueryPost sets: query, total_results, limit, results — no 'status' field
Every other success handler (handleRelationsForType, handleFullSketchForType, handleRelationQueryForPattern, handleConcordanceExamples, all ExplorationHandlers) sets response.put("status", "ok")
issues.suggestion: Add response.put("status", "ok") to handleBcqlQueryPost's success path, consistent with all other endpoints.

issues.summary: Concordance-examples response uses 'limit_requested' while BCQL response uses 'limit' for the same concept
evidence: handleConcordanceExamples: response.put("limit_requested", limit) — line ~305
handleBcqlQueryPost: response.put("limit", limit) — line ~382
Both fields represent the maximum number of results requested by the caller
issues.suggestion: Standardise on one name — 'limit' is shorter and matches the parameter name — and apply it consistently in both handlers.

issues.summary: QueryExecutor.findCollocations and executeSurfacePattern have identical Java signatures and return type but incompatible pattern format conventions
evidence: findCollocations(String lemma, String cqlPattern, double minLogDice, int maxResults): List<WordSketchResult> — expects CQL with %s placeholder or bracket-suffix form
executeSurfacePattern(String lemma, String bcqlPattern, double minLogDice, int maxResults): List<WordSketchResult> — expects BCQL with labeled 1:/2: positions
The distinction between CQL and BCQL pattern conventions is only communicated via Javadoc, not via the type system
issues.suggestion: Introduce a thin wrapper type (e.g. CqlPattern vs BcqlPattern, or a Pattern record with a format enum) so the compiler enforces correct pattern format at each call site, eliminating the silent mis-routing risk.

root_causes: response_shape_divergence: the surface-sketch relation entry was never aligned to the shape used by the single-relation and DEP branches as new code paths were added
overloaded_interface: findCollocations and executeSurfacePattern evolved independently for different callers without a type-level convention to distinguish their input formats
